Name: Autobailer Control Panel
Short description: Control panel for the Autobailer Machine.
Description: The Autobailer control panel is the brains of the machine. The programable logic controller (PLC) inside the panel reads data from the sensors and controls the motor automatically. The panel with the cellular modem can send data to an online platform. This allows for wireless monitoring and control of an Autobailer from anywhere with an internet connection. \n \nNOTE: You need to purchase an Autobailer Wireless Service plan for each Control Panel w/ Cellular Modem you purchase.

Name: Autobailer Installation Service
Short description: Select this option and we'll install an Autobailer on your well.
Description: NOTE: Autobailer Installation Service is only available in Oklahoma. \n \nAdd this option for each system you need installed and our technicians will get it done. Service is for installation of one Autobailer system. We will contact you to set a date a location for installation.

Name: AutoBailer Machine
Short description: The most affordable artificial lift on the planet.
Description:
We offer two types of machines. Part-time and full-time. The part-time unit is built to be powered by a mobile pumping trailer. This option makes it possible to run an Autobailer on a well that does not have power nearby. The full-time unit is designed to draw power from a disconnect coming from a power line. \n \nBoth types come with all the mechanical parts needed to operate an Autobailer: \n
    \n
  • Casing head adapter.
    • \n
  • Tray and mounting arm.
    • \n
  • A 1HP motor.
    • \n
  • A reel with 2000' of plastic coated steel cable.
    • \n
  • Canister seating assembly.
    • \n
  • A 20' canister.
    • \n
  • Sensors.
    • \n
  • Plumbing components.
    • \n
  • Air compressor (full-time only).
    • \n

Name: Autobailer Proximity Sensor
Short description: Replacement proximity sensor for the Autobailer.
Description: Replacement proximity sensor for the Autobailer.

Name: Autobailer Solenoid Valve
Short description: Replacement solenoid valve for the Autobailer.
Description: Replacement solenoid valve for the Autobailer.

Name: Autobailer Wireless Service
Description: This subscription includes the wireless bill for the Autobailer and the use of our OnPing web platform. One subscription must be added for each wireless Autobailer.

Name: Flow Switch
Short description: Detects liquid flow for the Autobailer Machine.
Description:
The Flow Switch is used to detect when liquid in being moved by the Autobailer Machine. When used with a cellular capable machine it makes it possible to monitor flow from afar and tell if a dry run occurs. \n
    \n
  • Get real time flow rates, straight to your computer, when used with the wireless control panel.
    • \n
  • Used to estimate how much liquid an Autobailer produces.
    • \n
  • Helps with problem detection and diagnostics.
    • \n

Name: Hand Held Control
Short description: Human machine interface (HMI) for the Autobailer.
Description:
\n
  • This control can be moved from one machine to another.  Allows the user to control the operation of a machine with or without wireless package.
    • \n
  • Can be used to set parameters of machine.
    • \n
  • When it is removed, the machine will run independently.
    • \n
  • Controller is necessary for machine installation.
    • \n

Name: Mobile Pumping Trailer
Short description: Used to power and pump multiple wells.
Description:
The Mobile Pumping Trailer allows intermittent pumping of multiple wells that have a part-time Autobailer Machine installed. \n
    \n
  • Includes a trailer, generator, compressor, and tank.
    • \n
  • Saves the cost of the Control Panel and electrical power connections for each well pumped this way.
    • \n

Description: The ball and seat make up the inner workings of the valves on a pump. They go inside the cages. The balls always go on top of the seat.

Name: Barrel - RH - Brass NiCarb
Description: The barrel is the central piece to the body of a pump. It keeps a seal around the plunger as the plunger strokes through the pump. It is a long tube with the same thread type on each end. RW (thin wall) barrels have box threads. RH (heavy wall) barrels have pin threads.

Name: Bushing - Seat - Barrel Cage - Flat - Brass
Description: Only used on top hold down (RHA) style pumps. This part connects the barrel cage or double valve cage to the gas anchor or strainer nipple. The pin threads on top screw into the barrel cage/double valve cage. The box threads on bottom screw into the gas anchor/strainer nipple.

Description: Only used on top hold down (RHA) style pumps. This part connects the barrel cage or double valve cage to the gas anchor or strainer nipple. The pin threads on top screw into the barrel cage/double valve cage. The box threads on bottom screw into the gas anchor/strainer nipple.

Name: Bushing - Seating - Brass
Description: Only used on top hold down (RHA) style pumps. This part connects the seating assembly to the barrel/extension. The box threads on top scew into the bottom of the mandrel. The pin threads on bottom screw into the barrel or extension.

Name: Bushing - Seating - Stainless

Name: Bushing - Valve Rod - Brass
Description: The valve rod bushing it the upper most piece of the plunger assembly. It allows the sucker rods to be connected to the valve rod. The top pin threads fit a sucker rod coupling. The bottom box threads fit the valve rod.

Name: Bushing - Valve Rod - Steel
Description: The valve rod bushing it the upper most piece of the plunger assembly. It allows the sucker rods to be connected to the valve rod. The top pin threads fit a sucker rod coupling. The bottom box threads fit the valve rod.

Name: Cage - Closed - Double Valve - Flat - Brass
Description: Along with a ball and seat this is the secondary valve on a barrel or plunger assembly. The pin threads on top fit the closed cage. The box threads on bottom fit a mandrel or seat plug. Typically, sizes 1 1/4" to 1 3/4" are for the plunger assembly and sizes 2" and 2 1/2" are for the barrel assembly.

Name: Cage - Closed - Double Valve - Flat - Stainless
Description: Along with a ball and seat this is the secondary valve on a barrel or plunger assembly. The pin threads on top fit the closed cage. The box threads on bottom fit a mandrel or seat plug. The top side connects to the valve rod guide or sliding sleeve cage and the bottom side connects to the barrel or extension.

Name: Coupling - Valve Rod to Pin End Plunger - Brass
Description: Part of the plunger assembly. Connects the valve rod to the plunger and allows fluid to pass through it. The top threads fit the valve rod. The bottom threads fit the plunger.

Name: Coupling - Valve Rod to Pin End Plunger - Stainless
Description: Part of the plunger assembly. Connects the valve rod to the plunger and allows fluid to pass through it. The top threads fit the valve rod. The bottom threads fit the plunger.

Name: Coupling - Valve Rod to Pin End Plunger - Steel

Name: Cup - Seating - Plastic - #100
Description: Part of the seating assembly, cups fit around the mandrel and are held in place by the spacers and locknut. When installed in a well they seal with the seating nipple. Typically 3 are used - a +30, a +45, and a + 70. These types vary slightly in size. With the +70 on top and the +30 on bottom the seating assembly will have a taper. This will make the best seal with the seating nipple.

Name: Extension - Brass
Description: Part of the barrel assembly, extensions connect to either end of a heavy wall (RH) barrel. The purpose is to give the ends of the plunger room to slide out of the barrel at the top and bottom of the stroke. This is known as a stroke through pump.

Name: Extension - Steel
Description: Part of the barrel assembly, extensions connect to either end of a heavy wall (RH) barrel. The purpose is to give the ends of the plunger room to slide out of the barrel at the top and bottom of the stroke. This is known as a stroke through pump.

Name: Guide - Valve Rod - Box End - Brass
Description: Part of the barrel assembly. Made so that the valve rod can fit through. This part guides the valve rod as the pump strokes while also allowing fluid to pass through. It can have pin threads to fit directly into a barrel (RWB) or box threads to fit a mandrel (RHA).

Name: Guide - Valve Rod - Box End - Stainless
Description: Part of the barrel assembly. Made so that the valve rod can fit through. This part guides the valve rod as the pump strokes while also allowing fluid to pass through. It can have pin threads to fit directly into a barrel (RWB) or box threads to fit a mandrel (RHA).

Name: Mandrel - Cup Type - Stainless

Name: PARKER Hydraulic Tank Desiccant Breather
Description: Desiccant breathers for hydraulic tanks can be added to existing hydraulic systems to add new functionality and to remove moisture from incoming ambient air. They can also be used to replace existing filters that are no longer active. Filters should be changed when the desiccant color has changed from blue to pink. Moisture in hydraulic fluid can change viscosity, affect flow rates, and cause vibration that can damage system components. Use desiccant breathers in hydraulic systems that encounter high temperature swings and humidity, such as paper and pulp processing, gear boxes, transformers, and mobile equipment.

Name: Flowline Scrapers - Dissolvable
Description:
Dissolvable flowline scraper balls are hands down the most cost effective way to remove paraffin buildup in pipes. They are easy to launch and don't require a catcher. The scraper balls can go around 90 degree turns that may stick rubber or plastic pigs. They are very inexpensive compared to rubber or plastic pigs or hot-oil treatments. Scraper balls do not interfere with other production and pipeline chemicals. \n \nThe balls contain paraffin inhibitor and pour point depressant. The residual traces of inhibitor help prevent re-deposition of paraffin for a short period of time. The pour point depressant helps the oil that contacts the pig flow easier at lower temperatures. \n \nThe dissolving rate of the balls is a function of fluid temperature, relative fluid movement, friction, and absorbability of the crude. These conditions vary greatly with each system. The melting point is about 145F. Scraper balls will dissolve in fluids with temperatures below their melting point (just at a slower rate). Some fluid movement past the pig is necessary to dissolve the pigs if the fluid temperature is below the pig’s melting point. \n \nThe number of scraper balls to be used is based on the amount of accumulation, length of pipe, temperature of fluid and other factors. Field experience indicate and that in short lines (500 feet or less) usually require 1 to 5 balls and longer lines (500 feet or more) usually require 5 to 10 or more balls. Regular periodic pigging is recommended to keep lines clean and prevent paraffin from becoming too hard or thick to remove. \n \nPROCEDURE \n \nInsert scraper balls (one at a time) through a launcher then pump through system and continue running balls (one at a time) into the line until: \n
    \n
  1. no additional decrease in flowing pressure or
    2. \n
  2. no additional increase in flowing rate or
    3. \n
  3. no additional accumulation is removed downstream.
    4. \n
\nIf desired, the amount of fluid pushing the ball can be measured to determine it's location. Do not use more than one ball at a time. ALWAYS record the volume of the fluid behind the ball as “insurance” so that it's location can be calculated should it become stuck. Applying heat to the pipe can melt the ball. ALWAYS have the capability to pump from the opposite end of the lines to free an obstruction. \n \nIMPORTANT \n \nScraper balls should be grooved on edges if there is a possibility that the ball could become stuck. This would allow some fluid movement past the edges. \n \nScraper balls can also be drilled in the center from each end; but leave an area in the center that is not drilled. This allows circulation to be established by raising pressure and breaking un-drilled portion of ball (center of the ball). \n \n

Name: Packer - AS III RH Auto
Description: The AS-III Packer is a single-grip packer with no upper hold-down for use where no differential pressure from below is present. This packer also features a large by-pass area to prevent swabbing when running or retrieving. This packer is ideal for isolating casing holes or perforations when used as the lower packer in conjunction with the Snapset Packer as the upper packer.

Name: Packer - Snapset II
Description:
The Snapset II Packer is a compression set tool requiring only straight set down weight to pack-off. This packer is run above another compression set packer (such as the AS-III Packer) to selectively treat, produce or inject in multiple zone completions. It is also used to isolate casing hole perforations.
\n
\nThis packer features a large by-pass area to prevent swabbing when running and retrieving. Once the packer is set, pressure from above or below acts down on the valve to maintain the seal and prevent upward movement of the tubing.
\n
\nWhen releasing, the valve allows debris to be washed from the upper slips. This packer is equipped with an internal latch to prevent setting prematurely when running in the hole. When releasing, the latch re-engages to allow movement downhole.

Name: ABSORB N DRY Floor Dry 40 lb. Bag
Short description: Absorb-N-Dry is an all-purpose sanitary maintenance product that is efficient and easy to use. It enhances safety and cleanliness in the workplace.
Description: Simply sprinkle Absorb-N-Dry granules generously over soiled area, let stand for a short time, then sweep up. For particularly stubborn spills or stains use a stiff broom to work the granules over the area, then sweep up. For flowing liquids, pile Absorb-N-Dry around the area creating a dam. Always use good housekeeping procedures and dispose of spent granules in accordance with local, state and federal regulations.

Name: Phillips 66® Omniguard 220 #2 Grease
Short description: A quality #2 grease developed for wet or corrosive environments.
Description:
Phillips 66® Omniguard 220 is a high performance, semi-synthetic, multipurpose, extreme-pressure (EP), corrosion preventive grease specially developed for use in industrial and automotive equipment operating in wet or corrosive environments. It is recommended for equipment exposed to water contamination, saltwater corrosion and/or high temperatures.
\n
\nOmniguard 220 is manufactured with high-quality base oils and a non-soap, calcium sulfonate thickener. It is fortified with EP and anti-wear additives as well as rust and corrosion inhibitors. It offers superior protection against rust and corrosion in high humidity, high-temperature applications such as those found in the steel, lumber, tire manufacturing, and paper industries. It has excellent load-carrying capacity, high shear stability and outstanding resistance to water washout.
\n
\nOmniguard 220 is NLGI GC-LB certified for use as a multipurpose automotive wheel bearing and chassis lubricant.

Name: CITGO Hydurance AW 32
Short description: Quality and affordability combined make this hydraulic oil an excellent choice.
Description: CITGO HyDurance AW Fluids are superior anti-wear hydraulic and circulating oils specially formulated to provide outstanding resistance to sludge formation, are chemically stable, and exhibit excellent anti-wear protection and filterability. Formulated with high quality base stocks and premium additive components to offer trouble-free service in high-pressure, high-output industrial hydraulic circuits. Recommended for service in industrial and mobile hydraulic sytems when used in accordance with equipment manufacturers' recommendations. They are designed to provide maximum service life to vane, piston, and gear pumps as well as other circuit components such as motors and servos.

Name: CITGO Premium Gear Oil 80/90
Description: CITGO Premium Gear Oils (MP) are multipurpose gear lubricants whose high-performance level is obtained by using a sulfur-phosphorus additive system. The selected additives impart extreme pressure properties to protect automotive gears which may be operated under the most severe service conditions encountered in passenger cars, trucks, farm tractors, earth moving, construction and other heavy-duty equipment. These additives specifically impart antiweld, antiscuff and antiwear properties necessary in heavy-duty service gear lubricants. Blended to meet the multigrade viscosity requirements described in SAE J306 viscosity recommended practice. These gear lubricants are formulated with naturally high VI, highly refined base oils and do not require viscosity index improvers to meet multigrade viscometric properties. These lubricants maintain their viscometric properties throughout their useful life and will not lose viscosity through shear.
